data.body: LANDING 7/10 closed+signed (ac0e,e1f2,74d9,24ec,f32e,a37b,9f25). Completion-verifier tail is REAL work + some over-strict nitpicks (the epic's own documented friction). a37b took 5 rounds: fixed genuine doc-sweep misses (config.md:100 false->true, plan-review-gate.md:368 'inert by default', adr/0002:91-93 stale opt-in narrative) + strengthened the default-on test to assert attestation REFRESH (AC2). 9f25 took 3 rounds: RAN the ADR-0036 A/B gate (separation 0.780>0.3, NIT 0.120<0.3 = PASS) and recorded it in derive_code_review_threshold.md; reconciled the adjudication JSONL schema by adding AC-named finding_id(=norm_id)+criterion(=joined criteria) to all 161 rows + the generator (kept canonical fields). KEY MECHANISM LEARNED: the completion-verifier close gate grounds against LOCAL HEAD (transition_close.py uses source=attested ref=HEAD); my manual 'rebar verify-completion' used the CLI default ref=origin/main (WRONG for un-landed stack) — always pass --ref HEAD to reproduce the close gate. Iterating fixes LOCALLY (fixup+autosquash into each leaf commit) without re-pushing to Gerrit each round; will re-push the final rewritten stack ONCE at the end, get fresh Verified+LLM, then submit. Remaining: b9c0,a8e5,d4cf closes.
